name: Dan Burley
abstract: Dan Burley (November 7, 1907, in Lexington, Kentucky – October 29, 1962, in Chicago, Illinois) was an American pianist and journalist. He appeared on numerous network television and radio shows in the US and had two radio shows of his own on WWRL Radio in New York.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Dan Burley, a reporter for a Negro newspaper in New York, "The Amsterdam News", who was also a pianist and exponent of a rhythm called scuffle. They formed the Basin Street Swing Club and for this session... part of the line-up was... myself

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I think it was Dan Burley that wrote this column in the Pittsburgh Courier... I used to read about them in the Pittsburgh Courier... was a guy who wrote for it named Dan Burley who used to write about all of the musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: He wrote a nice column about me. And he said he'd read it and he'd always heard about me and he wanted to meet me and shake my hand and thank me.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Dan Burley's column, uh..."Billie Holiday may work with Art Tatum at Cafe Society down in the Village" and...uh...news about Pigmeat Markham...

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: Outside of New Orleans? Dan Burley. Of course, Danny Barker outside of New Orleans, too.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: Outside of New Orleans? Dan Burley. Of course, Danny Barker outside of New Orleans, too.
